What is the time zone of Salt Lake City? Salt Lake City, Utah, falls within the Mountain Time Zone (MT). During standard time, which typically runs from early November to early March, Salt Lake City observes Mountain Standard Time (MST), which is 7 hours behind Coordinated Universal Time (UTC-7). However, during daylight saving time, which begins in the spring and ends in the fall, Salt Lake City adjusts its clocks forward by one hour, observing Mountain Daylight Time (MDT), or UTC-6. This means that from early March to early November, Salt Lake City is 6 hours behind Coordinated Universal Time.
